findings.description: Header logos/seal and signature image are missing from the HTML conversion.
findings.evidence: The PDF page shows the NC state seal and agency header at the top and a handwritten signature image above 'Kevin Hart'; the HTML page renders only text and omits these graphics.

findings.description: Footer agency logo graphic is missing from the HTML conversion.
findings.evidence: The PDF page includes a DEQ logo and footer branding at the bottom; the HTML shows footer text only, without the logo graphic.

findings.description: Cover-page layout spacing is compressed in HTML.
findings.evidence: The PDF cover page uses large vertical whitespace separating the centered title, right-aligned authority/due date, and receiving/submitting entity blocks; the HTML presents the same content in a more continuous top-to-bottom flow with reduced whitespace grouping.

findings.description: Cover-page alignment differs from the PDF.
findings.evidence: In the PDF, the title is centered and the statutory authority/due date block is positioned to the right; in the HTML these elements are left-aligned within the normal reading column.

findings.description: The HTML omits the full tabular content of page 1's 'Shallow Draft Navigation Channel Dredging Annual Report FY 2021-2022' and replaces it with an image placeholder, causing a significant loss of source text/data.
findings.evidence: PDF page 3 contains a full table titled 'Shallow Draft Navigation Channel Dredging Annual Report FY 2021-2022 G.S. 143-215.73F' with rows such as '7/14/2021 | 7956 Ocean Isle Beach | Ocean Isle | $420.00 | Channel Maintenance' and many subsequent entries through a total of '$9,543,427.32'. HTML only includes '' with no extracted table text.

findings.description: Table data integrity is broken for the dredging annual report table because the PDF table is not represented as structured HTML table data at all.
findings.evidence: PDF page 3 shows a 5-column table with headers 'Date | Project | Cooperator | Amount | Project Type' and dozens of rows. HTML has no corresponding <table> for this content; it is only an image placeholder.

findings.description: The final 'Project / Cooperators' table is incomplete in HTML; additional rows present in the PDF are missing due to truncation at the bottom of page 8.
findings.evidence: PDF page 10 shows the purple 'Project | Cooperators' table continuing beyond the visible rows, including entries after 'Mountain Island Reservoir'. HTML includes only 5 rows: 'Eno River', 'Jordan Lake - Folkners Branch', 'Lake Gaston', 'Lake Norman', and 'Mountain Island Reservoir', indicating the continuation of the table was not captured.

findings.description: Heading hierarchy is inaccurate: the document title is marked as an h2 instead of a single top-level h1, and the HTML uses multiple h2s without a top-level heading.
findings.evidence:
HTML begins main content with '

Annual Report on Projects Funded by the Shallow Draft Navigation Channel Dredging and Aquatic Weed

' followed by '

Intro

' and '

North Carolina Aquatic Weed Control Program - Funds Expended During the Fiscal Year

'. The PDF visually presents the report title as the primary title on page 2.

findings.description: A substantive text error changes the statutory citation wording from 'Statutes' to 'Statues'.
findings.evidence: PDF page 3 intro reads: 'NC General Statutes 143-215.73F(f) requires the Department of Environmental Quality...' HTML reads: 'NC General Statues 143-215.73F(f) requires the Department of Environmental Quality...'

findings.description: The HTML title line is truncated and omits the final word 'Fund'.
findings.evidence:
PDF page 2 title reads: 'Annual Report on Projects Funded by the Shallow Draft Navigation Channel Dredging and Aquatic Weed'. The correspondence letter on page 1 and surrounding context make clear the report name is for the 'Shallow Draft Navigation Channel Dredging and Aquatic Weed Fund'. HTML heading is '

Annual Report on Projects Funded by the Shallow Draft Navigation Channel Dredging and Aquatic Weed

', missing 'Fund'.

findings.description: The due date is incorrect in HTML.
findings.evidence: PDF page 2 shows 'Due Date: Oct. 1, 2021'. The cover letter on page 1 is dated 'October 1, 2022' and states the attached report covers FY 2021-2022. HTML preserves 'Due Date: Oct. 1, 2021', which appears inconsistent with the actual report submission date shown in the PDF package and may reflect a carryover issue from the source; however it matches the printed page-2 value and is not an HTML-only fabrication. No issue should be assigned here.
